question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user flood or slow the /api/v1/network/stake endpoint so that getNetworkStakeStatusCode() consistently returns a 5xx response, causing health() to short-circuit to Status.DOWN and suppress all staking reward anomaly alerts, allowing unpaid staking rewards to be silently stolen without operator detection?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the /network/stake REST endpoint to return a 4xx (non-5xx server error) response, forcing health status to Status.UNKNOWN instead of DOWN, so that the monitor silently ignores staking reward discrepancies while the cluster appears in an ambiguous state rather than alerting operators to theft?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the short-circuit logic in health() — where a non-UP restNetworkStakeHealth() result causes publishing() and subscribing() to be skipped entirely — to prevent the monitor from detecting that staking reward subscription scenarios have gone silent, enabling theft of unpaid staking rewards to go undetected?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: recordHealthMetric()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated health() calls that force CLUSTER_UP AtomicInteger to 0 by causing restNetworkStakeHealth() to return DOWN, thereby disabling the hiero.mirror.monitor.health gauge metric and suppressing Prometheus/Grafana alerts that would otherwise detect staking reward theft?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 5-second timeout in .timeout(Duration.ofSeconds(5)) by causing the /network/stake endpoint to respond in exactly 4.9 seconds on every health check, keeping the system perpetually in a degraded state where staking reward monitoring is suppressed without triggering a hard DOWN alert?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a TimeoutException on the /network/stake call (by making the endpoint hang beyond 5 seconds), which onErrorResume() maps to Status.DOWN, causing the cluster health gauge to drop to 0 and masking ongoing staking reward theft from monitoring dashboards?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a ConnectException on the /network/stake endpoint (e.g., by exhausting connection pools), which onErrorResume() maps to Status.DOWN, so that the CLUSTER_UP metric is set to 0 and staking reward theft proceeds undetected while operators believe the cluster is simply down?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a non-ConnectException, non-TimeoutException error in getNetworkStakeStatusCode() (e.g., a malformed response body), which onErrorResume() maps to Status.UNKNOWN rather than DOWN, keeping the cluster in an ambiguous health state that prevents automated alerting on staking reward theft?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHealthForZeroRate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the default failWhenInactive=false configuration in ReleaseHealthProperties so that getHealthForZeroRate() returns UNKNOWN instead of DOWN when publish/subscribe rates drop to zero, allowing staking reward theft to proceed while the monitor reports an ambiguous UNKNOWN status that does not trigger critical alerts?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribing()] Can an unprivileged external user cause all MirrorSubscriber subscription scenarios to report a rate of 0.0 via getRate(), so that subscribing() falls through to getHealthForZeroRate() returning UNKNOWN, and the monitor stops verifying staking reward transactions against the mirror node API, enabling theft of unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user cause all TransactionGenerator scenarios to report a rate of 0.0 via Scenario::getRate, so that publishing() returns getHealthForZeroRate() (UNKNOWN by default), and the monitor ceases to publish staking-related transactions, preventing detection of staking reward theft?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reactive chain in health() — where publishing().switchIfEmpty(subscribing()) is only evaluated when restNetworkStakeHealth() returns UP — to create a race condition where the /network/stake endpoint alternates between UP and DOWN, causing the monitor to intermittently skip staking reward subscription checks and miss theft events?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: recordHealthMetric()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the static CLUSTER_UP AtomicInteger shared across all instances to cause a persistent 0 value by repeatedly triggering health() calls that return non-UP status, disabling the cluster health metric and allowing staking reward theft to go unmonitored?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario where the /network/stake endpoint returns HTTP 200 with a stale or incorrect staking reward payload, causing restNetworkStakeHealth() to return UP (since it only checks the status code via is2xxSuccessful()), while the underlying staking reward data is manipulated to facilitate theft?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that restNetworkStakeHealth() only checks the HTTP status code (not the response body) of /network/stake, so that a response with HTTP 200 but empty or zeroed staking reward data is treated as healthy, masking theft of unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the Mono reactive pipeline in health() to emit no items (empty Mono) by making both publishing() and subscribing() return empty, so that doOnNext(this::recordHealthMetric) is never called and CLUSTER_UP retains a stale value of 1, falsely indicating the cluster is healthy while staking reward theft occurs?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribing()] Can an unprivileged external user cause mirrorSubscriber.getSubscriptions() to return an empty Flux (no subscriptions), so that subscribing() falls into switchIfEmpty(getHealthForZeroRate()) returning UNKNOWN, and no staking reward subscription verification occurs, enabling theft of unpaid staking rewards to go undetected?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user cause transactionGenerator.scenarios() to return an empty Flux, so that the reduce(0.0, ...) produces 0.0 and filter(sum -> sum <= 0) passes, causing publishing() to return getHealthForZeroRate() (UNKNOWN), which prevents staking reward transaction publishing and masks theft?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user perform a denial-of-service on the REST Java API backing /network/stake, causing persistent 503 responses that map to Status.DOWN, so that the CLUSTER_UP gauge stays at 0 for extended periods and staking reward theft is never detected by automated monitoring?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that health() re-evaluates restNetworkStakeHealth() on every call without caching, so that by intermittently causing /network/stake to fail, the attacker can create a flapping health status that prevents operators from trusting alerts about staking reward theft?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the error message interpolation in String.format('Network stake status is %s with error: %s', status, e.getMessage()) to inject misleading content into monitor logs via a crafted error message from the /network/stake endpoint, causing operators to misdiagnose staking reward theft as a benign infrastructure issue?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the ExceptionUtils.getRootCause(e) call to return an unexpected exception type (neither ConnectException nor TimeoutException), so that status defaults to UNKNOWN instead of DOWN, keeping the cluster in an ambiguous state that suppresses staking reward theft alerts?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribing()] Can an unprivileged external user cause all subscription scenarios to report exactly 0.0 rate via Scenario::getRate, so that the filter(sum -> sum > 0) in subscribing() never passes, and the monitor never emits UP status for staking reward subscription verification, allowing theft to proceed undetected?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reactive switchIfEmpty() chain in health() — where subscribing() is only called when publishing() returns empty — to create a scenario where publishing() always returns a non-empty Mono (UNKNOWN/DOWN) and subscribing() is never evaluated, permanently disabling staking reward subscription monitoring?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: recordHealthMetric()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the null check in recordHealthMetric() — where a null health object defaults to Status.UP — to cause CLUSTER_UP to be set to 1 even when the health pipeline emits null, falsely indicating the cluster is healthy while staking reward theft occurs?
